title: Potential Dangling DNS / Subdomain Takeover Candidate
description: A DNS name appears to reference an external provider target that is no longer resolving normally, creating a possible dangling record scenario.
impact: Dangling provider-backed DNS records can allow third parties to claim infrastructure behind a trusted hostname and serve attacker-controlled content.
likelihood: Medium to High when the target points to reclaimable SaaS or edge platforms.
recommendations: Verify whether the referenced external service is still owned and active.
Remove or repoint unused CNAME records and decommission stale integrations.
Continuously monitor provider-backed DNS names for dangling states.
